description: Add a Telegram gateway for desiBackend with OTP-based one-time verification, text request execution, clarification handling, execution-scoped artifact delivery, and profile-based routing for future API targets.

userStories.title: Create Telegram gateway schema and bootstrap integration
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I need Telegram gateway tables created through the same migration/bootstrap lifecycle as existing backend-managed tables so gateway data is durable and environment-consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: Add schema definitions and migrations for `telegram_identities`, `telegram_sessions`, `telegram_requests`, `telegram_dispatch_log`, and `profile_registry`
Migration path is wired into existing backend migration/bootstrap flow for admin/tenant databases
Schema includes indexes/constraints for lookup by `telegram_user_id`, `chat_id`, active request state, and idempotency keys
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement OTP verification policy for Telegram linking
userStories.description: As an unverified Telegram user, I want a secure OTP flow so I can verify once and unlock execution features.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement OTP issuance and verification flow tied to email and Telegram identity mapping
OTP validity is 10 minutes and maximum failed attempts are capped at 3 per verification session
After successful OTP verification, Telegram identity is linked to backend user and marked verified
Failed/expired OTP paths produce clear and safe user-facing error states
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add Telegram onboarding and unverified access blocking
userStories.description: As a Telegram user, I want `/start` onboarding and clear access boundaries so only verified users can submit execution requests.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement `/start` flow that initializes conversation state and prompts for email
Execution-related commands and plain text requests are blocked for unverified users
Blocked responses provide actionable guidance to complete verification
Verified users transition to ready state for request submission
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Seed default profile and implement profile commands
userStories.description: As a verified Telegram user, I want `/profiles` and `/use <profile>` so I can select how requests are routed now and in future expansions.
acceptanceCriteria: Seed `profile_registry` with at least one enabled `default` profile
Implement `/profiles` command returning available profiles with concise descriptions
Implement `/use <profile>` command that validates profile existence/enabled status and persists user active profile
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Build profile router with allowlisted target handlers
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I want request routing abstracted behind profile handlers so target APIs can evolve without changing Telegram UX.
acceptanceCriteria: Introduce internal profile router that resolves active profile to allowlisted handler
Implement default handler mapped to current create-and-execute backend flow
Prevent direct arbitrary endpoint selection from Telegram input
Request records persist selected `profileId` for auditability
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Enforce one active request lifecycle and profile switch lock
userStories.description: As a Telegram user, I want deterministic behavior so overlapping requests and mid-lifecycle profile changes do not corrupt flow state.
acceptanceCriteria: Reject new request submission if user has running execution or pending clarification
Reject profile switch attempts while user has active request lifecycle
User-facing responses clearly indicate why action was blocked
State transitions are persisted atomically to avoid race conditions
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Submit text requests and handle clarification loop
userStories.description: As a verified Telegram user, I want text requests and clarification prompts handled in chat so ambiguous goals can still execute successfully.
acceptanceCriteria: Ready-state plain text messages trigger create-and-execute request and persist `dagId`/`executionId` when available
`clarification_required` responses transition user state to awaiting clarification with request linkage
Clarification replies call resume-clarification API using original request context and profile
Loop continues until terminal success or failure without losing linkage to originating request
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement polling-based execution lifecycle notifications
userStories.description: As a Telegram user, I want concise lifecycle notifications so I know whether my request is accepted, completed, or failed.
acceptanceCriteria: Polling worker monitors execution status until terminal state
Send only major lifecycle messages: accepted, clarification-needed, completed, failed
Completion and failure messages include execution identifier
Polling cadence and timeout configuration are environment-driven
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Enforce execution-scoped artifact retrieval
userStories.description: As a Telegram user, I want artifact results limited to my current execution so I never receive files from unrelated runs.
acceptanceCriteria: Artifact selection pipeline accepts current `executionId` and filters results strictly to that execution
No artifacts from other execution IDs are eligible for dispatch
If required, augment backend artifact query path to support execution-scoped retrieval efficiently
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Deliver artifacts as Telegram attachments with link fallback
userStories.description: As a Telegram user, I want output artifacts sent directly as files, with safe fallback when files are too large for Telegram.
acceptanceCriteria: Dispatch artifacts as Telegram file attachments by default
When file exceeds Telegram limits, send signed download link with expiration
User receives concise summary before or alongside artifact payload
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add idempotent outbound dispatch and audit logging
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I want resilient and auditable delivery so retries do not duplicate user-visible messages and operations remain traceable.
acceptanceCriteria: Use dispatch idempotency keys for lifecycle messages and artifact sends
Webhook/polling retries do not produce duplicate Telegram messages for same event
Audit logs include onboarding, request dispatch, clarification events, completion/failure, artifact delivery, and profile used
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Apply gateway security controls and rate limits
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I want strong gateway security defaults so Telegram integration cannot leak credentials or be abused.
acceptanceCriteria: Gateway never returns backend API keys or session tokens to Telegram clients
Add rate limiting for verification attempts, command handling, and request submission paths
Production webhook path validates Telegram secret token before processing updates
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
